body:
Closes part of #5323.

Adds slice for extracting lower-dimensional grids from existing grids:

```julia
sst_grid          = slice(grid, :, :, 1)   # 2D horizontal grid (z collapses to Flat)
cross_section     = slice(grid, :, 1, :)   # 2D x–z grid
column            = slice(grid, 1, 1, :)   # 1D vertical column
```

## Semantics

- A **single integer index** collapses that dimension to `Flat`; a **colon** (`:`) retains it with its size, halo, spacing, and topology unchanged.
- For `RectilinearGrid` / `LatitudeLongitudeGrid` the index *value* does not affect the result (horizontal and vertical coordinates are independent), so only *which* dimension is collapsed matters. The value-dependence noted in #5323 (curved grids) is left for a follow-up.

## Implementation

Built on the same `cpu_face_constructor_*` / `pop_flat_elements` machinery as `with_halo`, `similar`, and `on_architecture`, so **stretched coordinates are preserved** and the result round-trips like any reconstructed grid. Methods for `RectilinearGrid` and `LatitudeLongitudeGrid` (the latter retains `radius` and precomputed metrics).

## Motivation

This is the grid-level primitive behind exchange/surface grids in coupled models. In NumericalEarth the manual

```julia
sst_grid = RectilinearGrid(grid.architecture, size=grid.Nx, halo=grid.Hx,
                           x=(-10kilometers, 10kilometers), topology=(Periodic, Flat, Flat))
```

becomes `slice(grid, :, :, grid.Nz+1 → surface)`. It is also what enables an atmosphere–ocean exchange grid at a different resolution from one of the components.

## Tests

`test_grids.jl` covers both grid types: topology collapse, retained size/halo/architecture/eltype, preserved nodes, stretched-coordinate preservation (RG), and retained radius + finite latitude-dependent metrics (LLG). Validated on CPU.

## Not included (follow-ups)

- A `Field`-level `slice` (a 2D field viewing the `k`-level data) — needed next for the coupling interpolation.
- Curved-grid (`OrthogonalSphericalShellGrid`) support and the index-value semantics for those.
- Range indices (`slice(grid, :, :, 1:10)` → `Bounded` sub-grid).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: Add slice(grid, i, j, k) to extract lower-dimensional grids
